level_signals: Title is Associate Manager, reports to Senior Manager
Comp range $105,400–$155,000 base, consistent with mid-senior IC at DoorDash

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4-6 years of experience.  You've succeeded in a competitive, challenging environment

You have an extreme sense of ownership. You display extreme ownership for the products and business lines that you are responsible for. You never shy away from raising your hand to solve ambiguous problems and are able to motivate and empower your teammates to go above and beyond.
You're an analytical powerhouse.  You let data win arguments, and you're comfortable pulling your own data in SQL and modeling in Excel.
You're a builder. You enjoy building from the ground up and excel in creating structure and process.
You solve problems from first principles.  You're excited to solve problems in innovative ways, and you resist "this is how it's always been done" thinking.

You will get to the lowest level of detail to drive execution against your strategy, working cross-functionally with Product, Operations, Sales, and Engineering to bring initiatives to life and scale impact.
You will serve as a key point of contact for strategic partners, building strong relationships and partnering closely to grow their business and inform our approach.
You're persuasive. You are an effective communicator, speaking as if you're right and listening as if you're wrong. You meet people on their level.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have experience working with CPGs
You have experience scaling both logistics and software offerings
You have experience in managing or supporting a complex P&L.
You have experience in the tech industry launching new partners and new products.
You have a demonstrated interest in logistics, on-demand services, and marketplaces.

scope.surface: DoorDash Drive CPG partnerships and go-to-market strategy
scope.business_model: B2B2C — logistics/fulfillment platform serving CPG brands and other enterprise partners
primary_metrics_inferred: Revenue / profitability of CPG business segment
core_responsibilities: Own the growth, profitability, and strategic roadmap for the CPG offering
Define and execute strategies to drive growth and adoption of key initiatives
Lead 0→1 initiatives and develop new growth opportunities
Partner with Product to define roadmap through data-driven insights and partner feedback
Work cross-functionally to bring initiatives to life and scale impact
Serve as a key point of contact for strategic CPG partners

green_flags: Clear business surface (CPG within DoorDash Drive) and ownership scope
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Role reports to a named level (Senior Manager), giving org context
red_flags: Location/remote policy is entirely unstated — candidates cannot determine office requirements or eligible geographies
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Wide comp band ($49,600 spread) with significant localization — actual offer could vary substantially by city
4-6 years experience range stated as a requirement but no other hard technical credentials, making it ambiguous who meets the bar

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Associate Manager', reporting structure, comp range, and years required)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description of DoorDash Drive serving CPGs)
location.type (not stated in JD; set to 'unclear')
location.work_auth (defaulted to 'open' as no restriction mentioned)
ambiguities: No work location or remote policy is stated anywhere in the JD body. The pay section references Illinois and Colorado ranges suggesting US-wide eligibility, but no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite designation is given.
The JD mentions 4-6 years of experience in a bullet under 'We're excited about you because…' rather than a formal 'Required' section, but the phrasing is unconditional and treated as hard here.
Compensation is described as 'localized according to an employee's work location' but no city-specific bands are provided, making the national range difficult to interpret for specific locations.
